name: Roast Turkey with Corn Bread-Chorizo Stuffing
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the turkey under hot water and pat it exterior and cavities completely dry with paper towels.
2. Spread the butter evenly on the turkey.
3. In a large pan, sauté onions and celery in remaining butter over medium heat until translucent. Remove with slotted spoon to a very large bowl.
4. Sauté the chorizo in the same pan until lightly brown. Remove to the same bowl and add the remaining stuffing ingredients. Toss to combine completely.
- If the mixture seems dry, add chicken stock or apple juice as needed.
5. Salt and pepper the turkey's cavity and loosely stuff it with the bread-chorizo mixture, leaving some room for expansion during cooking. Extra stuffing can be refrigerated in a covered casserole and baked at 375°F for 40 minutes.
6. Sew up and truss the turkey. Return to rack in roasting pan and turn turkey on its left side.
7. Add 1 cup stock and wine gravy to pan. Place in preheated oven at 400°F for 20 minutes.
8. Turn turkey onto its right side and continue roasting another 20 minutes.
9. Lower the oven temperature to 325°F. Turn turkey breast-side-down and roast for 1 hour and 20 minutes.
10. Baste every 15 minutes or so, adding stock as needed. Turn turkey breast-side-up and continue basting every 15-20 minutes.
- For a 12 lb turkey, cooking time will be from 15 to 20 minutes per pound; the internal temperature should reach 180°F. The juices in the thigh should run clear when pierced with a fork.
11. Remove turkey to a heated platter and rest. Skim off as much fat as possible from the pan drippings.
12. Transfer pan drippings, including brown bits, to a sauté pan. Reduce to gravy over high heat.
13. Carve turkey. Serve gravy and dressing on the side.
Total cooking time: 3 - 4 1/2 hours
